novel_features.name: Delivery audit
novel_features.description: Fold a message and its interactions into a chronological delivery timeline; exit non-zero on terminal failure.
novel_features.rationale: Bird returns the message and its delivery events as separate calls; merging them client-side is the only way to get a single audit row.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs a one-shot answer to 'did this SMS land?' with a non-zero exit on failure for pipeline gating.

novel_features.name: Failure cluster
novel_features.description: Aggregate recent message-interaction failures grouped by reason code.
novel_features.rationale: Bird has no aggregation endpoint; the answer only exists when interactions are mirrored into the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before paging humans about an SMS outage — it tells you whether the failures are clustered (one bad number, one carrier) or spread.

novel_features.name: SMS search
novel_features.description: Full-text search over message bodies, optionally filtered by sender or recipient phone number.
novel_features.rationale: FTS5 over the synced messages table joined to participants.identifierValue — no Bird endpoint takes a body substring.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to find a specific outbound SMS without scrolling pages of console output.

novel_features.name: CSV bulk dispatch
novel_features.description: Send a batch of SMS messages from a CSV with per-row idempotency keys, persisting the batch in the local store for later reconcile.
novel_features.rationale: The SDK leaves dispatch + reconcile as glue code; we ship both halves with idempotency.
novel_features.example: bird-pp-cli sms send-batch --csv recipients.csv --body-template "Hi {{name}}, your code is {{code}}" --dry-run
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for any batch larger than a handful of recipients where you need to know which ones failed afterward.

novel_features.name: Batch reconcile
novel_features.description: Re-fetch delivery interactions for every message in a batch, group failures by reason, and optionally retry.
novel_features.rationale: Pairs with send-batch. Reads the persisted batch, hits interactions per message, folds outcomes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this after every send-batch run — it answers 'how many landed?' and gives you a retry plan in one shot.

novel_features.name: Conversation timeline
novel_features.description: Render a conversation's messages, participants, and delivery interactions in canonical chronological order.
novel_features.rationale: SQL join over four local tables; no Bird endpoint returns this composite view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when triaging a customer thread — one command shows the full back-and-forth plus per-outbound delivery state.

novel_features.name: Cross-channel customer history
novel_features.description: List every message exchanged with one phone number across all conversations.
novel_features.rationale: Joins participants (by identifierValue) → conversations → messages locally — the API only lists by channel or conversation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs the complete back-and-forth with one customer regardless of which conversation it lived in.

novel_features.name: Tenant readiness check
novel_features.description: Run an SMS-tenant readiness checklist across channels, channel-config, anti-spam, compliance keywords, and messageability with a single exit code.
novel_features.rationale: Sequences five Bird endpoints into a single agent-readable checklist — bot vendors run this 12-curl flow manually today.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during onboarding for a new workspace or when an existing tenant's outbound SMS suddenly drops to zero.

novel_features.name: Auto-block from STOP fires
novel_features.description: Scan local inbound messages for STOP-keyword fires within a time window; emit a CSV ready for bulk-add (or apply directly).
novel_features.rationale: Local FTS over inbound bodies; no Bird endpoint correlates STOP fires to block-list candidates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use weekly to keep the workspace block list synchronized with customer opt-outs without writing a Python script.

narrative.headline: Terminal-native CLI for Bird's Conversations and SMS APIs with offline search, batch reconcile, and a local SQLite mirror.
narrative.value_prop: bird-pp-cli wraps every Conversations endpoint plus the SMS-relevant pieces of Bird's Channels API, then layers on what the SDKs miss: send/reconcile pairs with idempotency keys, FTS5 over message bodies, a delivery audit that exits non-zero on failure, and a tenant-readiness checklist that replaces the 12-curl onboarding flow.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authentication uses Bird's `Authorization: AccessKey <key>` header. Set BIRD_API_KEY plus BIRD_WORKSPACE_ID (every Bird endpoint is workspace-scoped) before calling any command. An optional BIRD_CHANNEL_ID provides a default for SMS commands so you don't have to pass --channel-id every time.

quickstart.comment: Check that BIRD_API_KEY and BIRD_WORKSPACE_ID resolve and the API is reachable.
Verify your SMS tenant is ready: channels, anti-spam, compliance keywords, and messageability all probed in one shot.
Inspect the request without sending; drop --dry-run to actually fire.
Find the most recent OTP-shaped SMS sent to that recipient. Works offline once 'bird-pp-cli sync' has populated the local store.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm BIRD_API_KEY is set and the access key is enabled in the Bird dashboard at app.bird.com/settings/security/access-keys.
Set BIRD_WORKSPACE_ID — every Bird path is workspace-scoped. Find it in the Bird dashboard under Settings → Workspaces.
Bird allows 50 retrieval calls/sec burst, 2000/min steady. The CLI's adaptive limiter respects 429s; retry after the suggested backoff or wait one minute.
Run bird-pp-cli sync first — search reads from the local store, which is empty until the first sync.
Bird lags interactions a few seconds behind the message create response; retry after 10 seconds or set --wait.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for bird-pp-cli when an AI agent or operator needs to interact with a Bird workspace from a terminal: sending one-off transactional SMS, batching reminders with reconcile, triaging open conversations, auditing message delivery, or maintaining the workspace allow/block list. Prefer this over raw curl when you want offline search, agent-shaped JSON, or a single command that folds multiple Bird endpoints into one answer.

recipes.title: Bulk-send an OTP campaign with reconcile
recipes.command: bird-pp-cli sms send-batch --csv recipients.csv --body-template "Your code is {{code}}" --apply --json
recipes.explanation: Sends one SMS per CSV row with a deterministic idempotency key and persists the batch in the local store. The returned JSON includes the batchId; pass it to 'bird-pp-cli sms reconcile <batchId> --json' afterward to re-fetch delivery interactions and group failures by reason.

recipes.title: Audit one message
recipes.command: bird-pp-cli messages audit msg_abc --channel-id ch_sms_1 --json --select status,events
recipes.explanation: Audit folds the message + interactions into a single timeline with a non-zero exit on terminal failure so it works as a CI gate or a Slackbot trigger.

recipes.title: Find an OTP we sent yesterday to one customer
recipes.explanation: Runs FTS5 over the local store; works offline once sync has run, no Bird API call. The --select narrows the response so an agent doesn't burn context on full message envelopes.

recipes.title: Onboard a new SMS tenant
recipes.explanation: Runs the five-endpoint readiness checklist (channel exists, channel-config enabled, anti-spam on, compliance keywords configured, messageability green) and exits non-zero with the failing check.

recipes.title: Sync compliance opt-outs into the block list
recipes.explanation: auto-block scans local inbound messages for STOP/HELP/UNSUBSCRIBE and emits a structured candidate list. With --apply it calls workspace rules bulk-add internally to write the block rules in one shot. Drop --apply for a print-only dry run.
